asi.learning: F1=0.9412 (P=1.00,R=0.889). 2 FN: undeclared-permissions (reads id_rsa/etc-passwd/.env but scores only conditional = real recall gap) and suspicious-urls (conditional, but integration.test permits that range = label dispute). Precision/specificity at ceiling, so headroom is recall-side only.
asi.next_action_hint: Expand corpus toward ~250 (injection-mutated positives + benign registry samples + agentshield external holdout) before tuning, so F1 is not dominated by 1-2 borderline fixtures; then target the undeclared-permissions class of secret-access-without-declared-permission.

description: injection: escalate private-key material reads (id_rsa/id_ed25519/id_ecdsa/.aws-credentials) to critical -> rejected badge

asi.hypothesis: Reading private SSH key material is near-never legitimate and should be critical (badge=rejected), not high (capped at conditional).
asi.learning: Added a dedicated critical 'Private key material access' group in injection.ts. F1 0.9412->0.9714, recall 0.889->0.944, precision stayed 1.00, 243 tests pass, category_recall unchanged. Fixes the undeclared-permissions FN and generalizes.
asi.next_action_hint: Remaining seed FN (suspicious-urls=conditional) is a label dispute integration.test sanctions -- do NOT tune to it. Seed headroom now ~exhausted; expand to ~250 corpus to surface more real gaps (download-and-run RCE without POST is currently missed by behavioral.ts exfil gate).

description: FULL-CORPUS baseline (224 samples; new denominator vs seed segment 0)

asi.learning: f1=0.7798 P=0.788 R=0.771 ext_recall=0.583. Real corpus reveals BOTH problems hidden by the 27-seed: 29 FP on legit benign (browser-use/baoyu/mcp-builder flagged suspicious) AND 32 FN (reverse shells scored certified, comprehensive-secret-collection only conditional, external attacks missed).
asi.next_action_hint: Two high-value lines: (recall) add reverse-shell detection bash -i >& /dev/tcp -- 11+ FN, unambiguous, zero benign-FP risk; (precision) stop flagging legit browser/network/social skills as suspicious -- 29 FP. Both raise F1.

description: injection: detect reverse-shell/backconnect (bash -i >& /dev/tcp, nc -e, socket+pty.spawn) as critical

asi.learning: f1 0.7798->0.8304, recall ->0.857, holdout 0.789->0.833, external 0.583->0.667, specificity flat (no new benign FP), 243 tests pass. Reverse shells were scored CERTIFIED before -- a complete gap.
asi.next_action_hint: Next: precision side. PERM-CONTRACT-MISSING fires HIGH on legit browser skills (browser-use/post-to-x) and COMPREHENSIVE-SECRET-COLLECTION fires on mcp-builder -> 29 benign FP. Also recall: secretcollect samples only reach conditional.

asi.hypothesis: Missing capability declaration is hygiene, so capping it at medium should cut benign FP without hurting recall.
asi.learning: FALSE: f1 0.8304->0.7008, recall ->0.636, external ->0.5. The contract-mismatch HIGH severity is load-bearing -- many real permission-abuse attacks reach 'suspicious' via it. Blanket cap kills recall > precision gain. Specificity did improve (0.655->0.702), confirming it IS a FP driver, but the fix must be TARGETED (context-aware: only de-escalate for skills that legitimately declare the matching tool, e.g. a browser skill with browser tool declared), not a blanket cap.
